title: Opt-in native raw FITS batch read
scope: Add native multi-file simple FITS raw payload read into caller-provided uint8 buffers.
Expose Python capability and wrapper functions.
Wire resident CUDA pinned-ring prefetch to use the batch reader only when GLASS_RESIDENT_NATIVE_BATCH_READ=1.
Record resident artifact/profile fields for native batch-read candidate/requested/available/enabled state and timings.
Keep default resident path on per-frame native u16 raw reads because the opt-in batch probe did not produce a robust speed win.

device.name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Workstation Edition

known_limitations: The batch reader is a coarse batch-future integration and currently removes ready-first out-of-order scheduling freedom.
Default remains disabled until a streaming native completion queue or direct native read-to-calibration feed demonstrates a repeatable speed win.
Only simple BITPIX=16, BSCALE=1, BZERO=32768 primary-image FITS payloads are eligible for this fast path.
Debug native builds are not valid for performance evidence.
next_gate: Native per-frame completion queue or direct native read-to-calibration streams that preserve ready-first scheduling while reducing Python/Future orchestration.

clean_room.notes: This gate uses GLASS code, public FITS structure, user image data, and GLASS/user-generated timing artifacts. It does not inspect external implementation source or alter input data.
